# llm-cost-optimizer-node

> Zero-config, drop-in client wrapper and proxy utility designed to automatically optimize prompts, reduce token counts, and cut LLM API costs.

---

# Core Integration Patterns

## 1. The Drop-In Wrapper (Recommended)

Intercepts all outgoing chat completion arrays automatically, compresses the payload locally via the in-memory engine, and triggers background metrics telemetry.

### Function

```javascript
wrapClient(clientInstance, config)
```

### Arguments

#### `clientInstance` (Object)

An instantiated OpenAI or compatible client object.

#### `config` (Object)

Object containing configuration keys.

| Key | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mode` | `String` | Options include `"chat"`, `"rag"`, `"agent"`, or `"codegen"` |
| `rapidApiKey` | `String` (Optional) | Secret registration identifier. Activating this parameter enables the asynchronous 5-second background batch telemetry interval. |
| `strategy` | `Array` (Optional) | Specific compression routines to run. Defaults run standard minification and mode-specific fallbacks. |
| `language` | `String` (Optional) | ISO dictionary language selector (`"en"`, `"es"`, `"fr"`). Defaults to `"en"`. |
| `ignore_words` | `Array` (Optional) | Array of explicit strings protected from minification rules. |

---

### JavaScript

```javascript
const { OpenAI } = require('openai');
const { wrapClient } = require('llm-cost-optimizer-node');

const openai = wrapClient(
    new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY }),
    {
        mode: "rag",
        rapidApiKey: process.env.RAPID_API_KEY
    }
);
```

---

# 2. Advanced Manual Mode

Direct access to the core compression class for handling raw text blocks, pre-processing RAG contexts, or saving tokens in custom vector workflows.

## Class

```javascript
LLMCostOptimizer
```

## Constructor Options

```javascript
{ apiKey }
```

## Instance Methods

```javascript
compress({ text, strategy, language })
```

---

### JavaScript

```javascript
const { LLMCostOptimizer } = require('llm-cost-optimizer-node');

const optimizer = new LLMCostOptimizer({
    apiKey: process.env.RAPID_API_KEY
});

const result = await optimizer.compress({
    text: "Long text block...",
    strategy: ["minify", "strip_stopwords"]
});

// Returns:
// {
//   compressed_text: "...",
//   metrics: {
//     original_tokens: X,
//     compressed_tokens: Z
//   }
// }
```

---

# Global Executable CLI Interface

## Command

```bash
llm-optimize benchmark
```

## Source Target

```text
./bin/cli.js
```

## Behavior

Executes a standardized processing matrix comparison, evaluating token estimations, local optimization effectiveness, and validating factual string security requirements via stdout tables.

---

# Optimization Strategies Reference

| Strategy | Description |
|---|---|
| `minify` | Strips line breaks, extra spaces, and redundant formatting structural noise. |
| `strip_stopwords` | Removes grammatical boilerplate filler terms (`the`, `is`, `and`) while locking down core factual intent nouns/verbs. |
| `stemming` | Inflects/reduces derived word roots down to structural linguistic bases. |
| `strip_boilerplate` | Strips raw HTML/XML markup anchors out of text streams. |

---

# Runtime Safety & Technical Architecture

## Offline-First Core

Intercepts `chat.completions.create` message payloads synchronously, parsing strings locally in-memory in `<2ms`.

---

## Non-Blocking Telemetry

Outgoing analytics are held in a memory ring buffer queue and batch-flushed to:

```text
https://llm-token-optimizer-api.vercel.app/api/telemetry
```

every 5 seconds asynchronously to achieve zero application thread degradation.

---

## Fail-Safe Execution

Network timeouts, expired keys, or gateway API errors are caught internally.

The wrapper prints a console warning and transparently forwards the untouched original prompt directly to the destination LLM provider.

Application uptime is preserved.
